models.generation_context: 2nd-generation XC40, introduced 2017; 2023+ refresh applied; B4 FWD reintroduced as base for 2026 MY to lower entry price
models.model_summary: Compact luxury crossover offered in mild-hybrid B4 (FWD, 194 hp) and B5 (AWD, 247 hp) flavors paired to an 8-speed automatic. For 2026, Volvo reintroduces front-wheel drive as the base configuration and adds an Ultra Black Edition; the all-electric variant is now sold separately as the EX40.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports' predicted-reliability outlook for the 2026 XC40 is below average, projected from 2023-2025 model-year data showing several recalls (rearview camera image, software issues) and owner complaints around the infotainment system. JD Power has not published a 2026-MY VDS score; the 2024-MY XC40 received a Quality & Reliability score of 74/100 (about average).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a model-specific APEAL score for the 2026 XC40. The 2024 model received high marks for driving comfort and styling but lower marks for technology user experience.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the XC40's distinctive Scandinavian styling, plush front seats, and intuitive Google-built-in infotainment, while criticizing tight cargo space, modest acceleration in B4 form, and an aging platform versus newer rivals. The reintroduced B4 FWD makes the value case stronger but the experience is still rated as a personality buy rather than a class leader.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise styling, comfort and safety features; common complaints center on touchscreen complexity, road noise, and software issues. KBB sample shows 88% would recommend.

trims.notes: B4 FWD newly reintroduced for 2026 as the entry powertrain after Volvo had moved the XC40 to AWD-only in 2024-2025. 48V mild-hybrid system treated as 'ice' per brand-spec rule (no electric-only drive). Volvo's destination fee for hybrid/ICE 2026 lineup is $1,295. Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch repeatedly; specs reconciled against fueleconomy.gov, IIHS, KBB, Edmunds. Android Auto historically not supported in Volvo Google-built-in vehicles; Apple CarPlay wired only. NHTSA has not yet rated the 2026 XC40. The XC40 did not earn an IIHS Top Safety Pick for 2026 despite good crashworthiness ratings.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and rear seats

changes: Adds Harman Kardon 13-speaker premium audio (vs. 8-speaker standard)
Adds leather seating surfaces (vs. synthetic leather)
Adds heated steering wheel and heated rear seats
Adds memory function on driver's seat

trims.notes: Images shared with B4 Core/B5 Core within the xc40-standard family.

changes: Adds B5 powertrain: 247 hp / 258 lb-ft (vs. B4 194 hp / 221 lb-ft)
Towing capacity increases to 3,500 lb (vs. 2,000 lb)

trims.notes: Images shared with xc40-standard family.

changes: Adds B5 AWD powertrain (247 hp / 258 lb-ft, vs. B4 194 hp / 221 lb-ft FWD)
Adds leather seating, heated rear seats and steering wheel

trims.notes: Images shared with xc40-standard family. Same drivetrain as B5 Core, with Plus equipment package.

changes: Adds B5 AWD powertrain (247 hp / 258 lb-ft)
Adds nappa leather upholstery with ventilated front seats

trims.notes: Top-tier non-special trim. Shares photography with xc40-standard family.

Singleton trim_family 'xc40-black-edition' per sole-trim atomic rule — distinctive blacked-out exterior treatment warrants its own image family. All 4 required image angles must be captured for this trim directly.

models.notes: Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch repeatedly during research; pricing reconciled from KBB (2026 XC40 trim pages), Edmunds, and Volvo's press release coverage. Volvo destination fee for 2026 hybrid/ICE lineup is $1,295 (per hybrid-lineup press release). EPA fueleconomy.gov verified: XC40 B4 = id 49470 (FWD), XC40 B5 AWD = id 49546. IIHS rated 2026 XC40 with Good crashworthiness but no Top Safety Pick award (didn't earn TSP/TSP+ designation). NHTSA has not rated 2026 XC40. Andro Auto historically not supported in Volvo Google-built-in vehicles. The 2026 XC40 received a notable update with the reintroduction of the front-wheel-drive B4 powertrain to lower entry pricing. The all-electric variant of the XC40 (formerly XC40 Recharge) is now sold separately as the EX40 in 2025+.

models.generation_context: 2nd-generation XC60, introduced 2017; significant 2026 mid-cycle refresh adding new 11.2-inch portrait infotainment screen, new front-end styling shared with XC90, and refreshed interior
models.model_summary: Midsize luxury crossover offered in mild-hybrid B5 (247 hp, AWD) and plug-in hybrid T8 (455 hp combined, ~35 mi electric range) forms, plus a 455-hp T8 Polestar Engineered range-topper with Ohlins dampers. For 2026 the XC60 receives a notable refresh: new 11.2-inch tablet-style center screen, restyled grille and lighting, refreshed interior trim, and adaptive cruise control made standard.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY VDS score for the XC60. Consumer Reports' predicted-reliability outlook is average. Volvo has issued several recent recalls (rearview camera, software) affecting 2020-2025 XC60 inventory.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY APEAL score for the XC60.

professional_reviews.summary: The 2026 mid-cycle refresh is praised for sharper styling and a much-improved central touchscreen, though Edmunds notes the 'iPad glued to the dash' visual style. The T8 plug-in remains a quick, refined family SUV with a useful 35-mile electric range, while the B5 mild hybrid is positioned as a comfortable but unremarkable daily. Owners gain standard adaptive cruise control across the lineup for 2026.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise interior quality, ride comfort, and the T8's electric range; common complaints concern infotainment responsiveness (improved for 2026) and quirky shifter ergonomics.

notable_other: New for 2026: 11.2-inch portrait center touchscreen (21% higher pixel density)

trims.notes: Base trim of ICE/mild-hybrid powertrain line. B5 = 48V mild hybrid I4, treated as 'ice' per brand spec (no electric-only drive). MSRP $50,995 (Volvo's quoted starting price) includes destination $1,395 = $49,600 base. Did not earn 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick (XC90 did, XC60 did not for 2026).

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and heated rear seats

changes: Adds leather seating surfaces (vs. synthetic leather)
Adds heated steering wheel and heated rear seats

Adds ventilated nappa leather seats with massage function

T8 = 2.0L turbo+supercharged I4 (312 hp) with 143 hp rear electric motor; total system output 455 hp / 523 lb-ft.

Gold seatbelts and unique interior trim

trims.notes: Singleton xc60-t8-polestar family per sole-trim rule (carries own 4 image angles). MSRP $81,095 starting (Edmunds) - $1,395 destination = $79,700 base. Polestar Engineered is the performance-tuned range-topper of the XC60 lineup; same 455 hp powertrain as T8 but with unique chassis.

models.notes: XC60 has two distinct powertrain lines per spec §6.4: B5 (48V mild hybrid, treated as 'ice') and T8 (plug-in hybrid). Both have separate base trims (B5 Core and T8 Core). 2026 receives notable mid-cycle refresh: new 11.2-inch portrait infotainment with Qualcomm Snapdragon, restyled grille shared with XC90, refreshed interior. EPA fueleconomy.gov verified: B5 AWD = id 49547, T8 AWD = id 49771. Volvo destination fee for 2026 hybrid lineup is $1,395. NHTSA has not rated 2026 XC60. IIHS did not grant Top Safety Pick to 2026 XC60 (XC90 and EX90 did receive 2026 awards). Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch; prices reconciled from KBB, Edmunds, US News, and the Volvo hybrid lineup press release coverage.

models.generation_context: 2nd-generation XC90 (SPA1 platform, introduced 2015) extended through 2026 alongside its eventual EV successor, the EX90. Received a major refresh for 2025 (new grille, cabin updates) carried into 2026; B6 supercharged mild-hybrid added; T8 PHEV continues with 32 mi electric range.
models.model_summary: 3-row luxury SUV offered in three powertrain lines: B5 mild-hybrid (247 hp), B6 supercharged mild-hybrid (295 hp), and T8 plug-in hybrid (455 hp combined, 32 mi electric range). All AWD. Comes in 7-passenger standard or 6-passenger captain's-chair second row. The XC90 received a 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick (both gas and PHEV versions).

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY VDS score for the XC90. Consumer Reports' predicted-reliability outlook is average. Aging SPA1 platform underpinning the XC90 is well-understood; T8 PHEV system has historically been a weak spot for reliability per CR.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY APEAL score for the XC90.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the XC90's classic Scandinavian styling, supremely comfortable cabin, and the T8 PHEV's 32-mile electric range, while noting the SPA1 platform is showing its age relative to newer rivals like the BMW X5 and Mercedes-Benz GLE. Edmunds called the 2025.5/2026 refresh adequate but called out the dated touchscreen architecture; T8 is a strong 3-row plug-in option for those not yet ready for the all-electric EX90.

owner_reviews.summary: Long-term XC90 owners consistently praise interior comfort, safety tech, and family-hauling capability; common complaints concern infotainment glitches and T8 charging-system bugs in earlier units.

trims.notes: Base trim of B5 mild-hybrid line. MSRP $62,345 (Volvo's quoted starting price for B5 Core 7-passenger AWD, KBB) - $1,395 destination = $60,950 base. B5 = turbocharged 2.0L with 48V MHEV, treated as 'ice' per spec. Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick (Midsize luxury SUV category). NHTSA has not rated 2026 XC90. 6-passenger configuration is optional within Plus/Ultra trims, not a separate trim entry per spec §6.4 (cab/equipment configurations at same price level are options).

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and heated rear seats

Adds memory driver seat and 360-degree camera

trims.notes: MSRP $65,550 starting (KBB, 6-pass; 7-pass slightly less) - $1,395 destination ≈ $64,155 base. Images shared with xc90-standard family.

B6 powertrain = 2.0L I4 with both turbocharger and supercharger, classified as 'twin_turbocharged' engine_config alternative; treated as 'ice' (48V MHEV). MSRP $66,150 (KBB) - $1,395 destination = $64,755 base. Same body as B5 but more powerful. Earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick alongside B5/T8 variants.

notable_other: Leather seating, heated rear seats, heated steering wheel

Adds memory driver seat, 360-degree camera, power tailgate

notable_other: T8 PHEV: 2.0L turbo+supercharged I4 (310 hp) + 145 hp rear electric motor = 455 hp combined

trims.notes: Base trim of T8 PHEV powertrain line. Volvo's hybrid press release: 'XC90 plug-in hybrid starting at $77,595 MSRP' (incl. destination) - $1,395 destination ≈ $76,200 base. Used KBB $76,200 starting and got $74,805 base. Note pricing source agreement between Volvo press ($77,595 with destination) and KBB ($76,200 with destination) differs by ~$1,395; using KBB. EPA T8 AWD fueleconomy.gov id 49772 verified. 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick (PHEV version).

notable_other: Heated rear seats and steering wheel

changes: Adds heated rear seats and steering wheel
Adds memory driver seat, 360-degree camera, power tailgate

trims.notes: MSRP $78,850 (KBB) - $1,395 destination = $77,455 base. Images shared with xc90-t8 family.

changes: Adds ventilated nappa leather with massage

trims.notes: MSRP $83,400 (KBB) - $1,395 destination = $82,005 base. Volvo also offers an Ultra Dark variant at similar price (Black Edition styling); documented here as standard T8 Ultra. Images shared with xc90-t8 family.

models.notes: XC90 has three distinct powertrain lines per spec §6.4: B5 (48V MHEV, 247 hp, 'ice'), B6 (supercharged + turbocharged 48V MHEV, 295 hp, 'ice'), and T8 (PHEV, 455 hp combined). All AWD. Each powertrain line has its own base trim (B5 Core, B6 Core, T8 Core). 7-passenger seating is standard; 6-passenger captain's-chair configuration is available on Plus and Ultra trims as a $500-ish option (treated as configuration option per spec §6.5, not separate trim entries). 'Ultra Dark' variants (blacked-out trim) priced same as Ultra are also folded as options. EPA fueleconomy.gov verified: B5 AWD = id 49611, B6 AWD = id 49610, T8 AWD = id 49772. The XC90 (both gas and PHEV) earned 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick. NHTSA has not rated 2026 XC90. Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch; prices reconciled from KBB and Volvo hybrid press release coverage.

models.generation_context: 2nd-generation V60 (introduced 2019); Cross Country (raised ride height, body cladding, standard AWD) is the only V60 sold in the US for 2026 after the standard V60 was discontinued. V60 Polestar Engineered also discontinued; V90 Cross Country discontinued for the US after MY25.
models.model_summary: Volvo's lone remaining gas-engine passenger car in the US for 2026 — a raised, rugged wagon with standard AWD, the B5 mild-hybrid powertrain (247 hp), and 7.9 in of ground clearance. Offered in Plus and Ultra equipment trims only. After V90 Cross Country was discontinued, the V60 Cross Country carries the entire Volvo wagon flag in North America.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS for the Volvo brand: 296 PP100 (industry avg 204). Ranked 2nd to last in 2026 VDS. Driven heavily by infotainment/electronics problems in Recharge/EV lineup. Ranked 15th of 25 in CR 2026 brand rankings - middle of pack. XC60 PHEV reliability is below average. No 2026-MY model-level data is published for this specific model; assessment is based on brand-level signals.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Volvo V60 Cross Country or for the Volvo brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers love the V60 Cross Country as one of the last upscale wagons sold in the US, praising its comfortable ride, standard AWD, and rare-on-the-road styling. Edmunds notes the platform is dated and infotainment is unchanged from older XC60, but the wagon's character makes it a niche favorite.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are sparse due to low sales; existing reviews skew positive on comfort, AWD capability, and styling.

notable_other: Standard AWD with Cross Country ground clearance (7.9 in)
Heated rear seats and steering wheel
9-inch portrait touchscreen (legacy infotainment, not the 11.2-inch refresh)

trims.notes: Base trim. V60 CC has only Plus and Ultra trims for 2026 — no Core. V60 CC destination fee is $1,295 (per spec note in pre-flight), differing from the rest of the 2026 lineup ($1,395). $52,300 (Edmunds starting MSRP including destination) - $1,295 destination = $51,005 base. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 49350 (2026 Volvo V60CC B5 AWD, 26 combined MPG). Did not earn 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick. NHTSA has not rated 2026 V60 CC. Uses Volvo's legacy 9-inch infotainment; not yet updated to the 11.2-inch screen rolled out to XC60/XC90 for 2026.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and rear seats

trims.notes: $57,600 (Edmunds starting MSRP) - $1,295 destination = $56,305 base. Images shared with v60cc-standard family.

models.notes: V60 Cross Country is the only V60 sold in the US for 2026; standard V60 sedan discontinued and V90 Cross Country dropped for 2026 MY. Single B5 (48V mild hybrid) powertrain line treated as 'ice' per brand spec. Destination fee $1,295 (lower than rest of 2026 Volvo lineup which is $1,395). Did not receive 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick. Still uses Volvo's legacy 9-inch portrait infotainment; not refreshed to the 11.2-inch screen of the 2026 XC60/XC90. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 49350 verified. Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch; specs reconciled from Edmunds, KBB, and Volvo Cars St-Léonard technical sheet.

models.generation_context: 1st-generation EX30 (introduced as 2025 MY in the US after a one-year delay from global launch); built on Geely's SEA platform. 2026 adds the Single Motor Extended Range and an off-road-flavored Cross Country variant. Per CarsDirect reporting, EX30 is slated for discontinuation after 2026 MY.
models.model_summary: Subcompact luxury EV crossover — Volvo's smallest and most affordable. Offered in single-motor RWD (268 hp, 261 mi EPA range) and twin-motor AWD (422 hp, 253 mi) configurations. The new-for-2026 EX30 Cross Country adds 0.8 inches of ground clearance, skid plates, and all-terrain tires on a twin-motor AWD platform.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS for the Volvo brand: 296 PP100 (industry avg 204). Ranked 2nd to last in 2026 VDS. Driven heavily by infotainment/electronics problems in Recharge/EV lineup. Ranked 15th of 25 in CR 2026 brand rankings - middle of pack. XC60 PHEV reliability is below average. No 2026-MY model-level data is published for this specific model; assessment is based on brand-level signals.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Volvo EX30 or for the Volvo brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers generally praise the EX30's price-for-performance ratio and Scandinavian design, but consistently criticize the single-touchscreen-controls-everything cabin and missing physical buttons. Edmunds and Wards Auto both call out the new Single Motor as the smarter buy: less expensive, more efficient, and arguably the value sweet spot of the lineup. The Cross Country variant earns marks for off-road styling but loses 35+ miles of range.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews on the 2026 EX30 are limited; existing reviews praise styling and acceleration while criticizing infotainment ergonomics.

notable_other: Single 12.3-inch tablet-style display (no separate driver display)
Wireless Apple CarPlay (added via OTA software update in 2025)

trims.notes: Base trim of EX30 lineup. New-for-2026 RWD Single Motor configuration with 268 hp, ~261 mi EPA range. $38,950 starting MSRP per Volvo press release (includes destination) - $1,295 destination = $37,655 base; using $37,650 rounded. EPA MPGe values mirrored into fuel_economy per spec §3.6 v1.1. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 49989 verified. The EX30's all-glass-rooftop and tablet-only interior are signature design choices.

changes: Adds second front motor: AWD-electric, 422 hp (vs. 268 hp RWD)
Drops 0-60 from 5.1 sec to 3.4 sec (one of the quickest sub-compact EVs)
Reduces EPA range to 253 mi (vs. 261 mi)

trims.notes: MSRP $46,345 (Volvo press release, includes destination) - $1,295 destination = $45,050 base. Images shared with ex30-standard family.

features.sound_system: Harman Kardon premium soundbar (9-speaker with subwoofer)

changes: Adds Twin Motor AWD-electric powertrain (422 hp)
Adds upgraded Harman Kardon audio with subwoofer

trims.notes: MSRP $48,045 (Volvo press release, includes destination) - $1,295 destination = $46,750 base. Images shared with ex30-standard family.

features.sound_system: Harman Kardon premium soundbar (9-speaker with subwoofer)
notable_other: Cross Country body cladding, skid plates, raised ride height (+0.8 in vs. standard)

trims.notes: Sole-trim singleton trim_family 'ex30-cross-country' per spec §6.2/§7 — distinct off-road-styled body warrants its own image family with all 4 required angles. EX30 Cross Country sold only in this Twin Motor Performance Ultra Cross Country configuration. MSRP $48,150 starting (Volvo press release, includes destination) - $1,295 destination = $46,855 base. Cross Country body cladding, skid plates, +0.8 in raised ride height, all-terrain tires, unique 19-in wheels. Range falls to 227 mi due to wheel/tire setup. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 49991 (19-in wheel variant).

models.notes: EX30 has a single BEV powertrain group with two motor configurations (Single Motor RWD 268 hp; Twin Motor AWD 422 hp). Single Motor Plus is the lineup base; Cross Country variant uses Twin Motor and is in its own singleton trim_family per spec §7 (distinct body styling). EPA fueleconomy.gov verified: Single Motor 49989, Twin Motor Performance 49992, Cross Country 19-in 49991 (18-in CC variant = 49990 also exists, EPA 89 MPGe; the 19-in is what Volvo ships in the US). EX30 destination fee is $1,295 (Volvo lists EX30 prices include destination charges in press release format). Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch; specs reconciled from Volvo press release, Edmunds, Wards Auto, and EPA. Note that CarsDirect has reported EX30 will be discontinued after MY26; this is the current 2026 lineup.

models.generation_context: Compact luxury EV crossover; renamed from XC40 Recharge for 2025 MY. Shares CMA platform with the XC40. 2026 simplifies the lineup — Core trim dropped, Ultra Black Edition is the new range-topper, Single Motor RWD and Twin Motor AWD both continue.
models.model_summary: All-electric compact luxury SUV, formerly the XC40 Recharge. Offered in Single Motor RWD (248 hp, 296 mi EPA range) and Twin Motor AWD (402 hp, 260 mi range). For 2026, the lineup narrows to three trims — Single Motor Plus, Twin Motor Plus, and Twin Motor Ultra Black Edition — with the Core trim retired and Black Edition replacing the conventional Ultra.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY VDS score for the EX40. Consumer Reports' predicted reliability for the related XC40 Recharge has been below average, with software and 12V battery issues. EPA EX40 entry shares VIN history with XC40 Recharge.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY APEAL score for the EX40.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the EX40 as a 'good but not class-leading' compact EV — refined ride and cabin, but range trails the Tesla Model Y and Hyundai Ioniq 5 at similar price points. Edmunds and KBB note the 11-inch portrait infotainment is dated relative to the EX30 and EX90's larger tablet-style displays.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews are limited for the 2026 EX40; carry-over from XC40 Recharge complaints have centered on real-world range falling short of EPA estimates and infrequent software updates.

notable_other: Leather seating, heated steering wheel and rear seats

trims.notes: Base trim of EX40 lineup for 2026. Single Motor RWD with 248 hp / 310 lb-ft / 296 mi EPA range. MSRP $55,150 (Edmunds starting incl. destination) - $1,295 destination = $53,855 base. EPA MPGe mirrored into fuel_economy per spec §3.6 v1.1. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 49747 verified. Volvo's destination for EX40 is $1,295 per Edmunds reporting (consistent with EX30; not the $1,395 of XC60/XC90).

changes: Adds second front motor: AWD-electric, 402 hp (vs. 248 hp RWD)
Reduces EPA range to 260 mi (vs. 296 mi)

trims.notes: MSRP $56,900 (Edmunds starting) - $1,295 destination = $55,605 base. Images shared with ex40-standard family.

notable_other: Black Edition: gloss-black grille, badges, mirror caps, roof rails, 21-inch black wheels

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family 'ex40-black-edition' per spec §6.2/§7 — distinct blacked-out exterior warrants own image family with all 4 required angles. MSRP $60,850 (Edmunds starting incl. destination) - $1,295 destination = $59,555 base. For 2026 the conventional Ultra trim is dropped; Ultra Black Edition becomes the top trim.

models.notes: EX40 has a single BEV powertrain group with two motor configurations (Single Motor RWD 248 hp; Twin Motor AWD 402 hp). For 2026 Volvo simplified the lineup: Core trim retired, Ultra replaced by Ultra Black Edition (which sits in its own singleton trim_family). EX40 is the renamed-for-2025 XC40 Recharge — same platform, refreshed branding. EPA fueleconomy.gov verified: Single Motor 49747, Twin Motor 49749. NHTSA has not rated the 2026 EX40; IIHS did not award TSP/TSP+. EX40 destination $1,295 (consistent with XC40 and EX30, not the $1,395 of XC60/XC90).

models.generation_context: 1st-generation EX90 (SPA2 platform, introduced as 2024 MY); shares platform with Polestar 3. 2026 brings major upgrades: new 800-volt electrical architecture (cuts DC fast charge time, adds 155 mi in 10 min), a new single-motor RWD base trim ($76,695 starting), and an uprated Twin Motor Performance making 670 hp (vs. 510 hp prior year).
models.model_summary: 3-row luxury electric SUV, Volvo's all-electric flagship. For 2026 gains an 800V architecture for faster DC charging, a new single-motor RWD entry trim, and an uprated 670-hp Twin Motor Performance variant. The 2026 EX90 won an IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award. Built alongside the EX90's mechanical twin, the Polestar 3, in Charleston, SC.

reliability.summary: EX90's first production year (2024) was plagued with software delivery delays; early units shipped with degraded features that were activated via OTA. Consumer Reports' predicted reliability remains below average; the 2026 800V upgrade may resolve some issues but track record is short.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY APEAL score.

professional_reviews.summary: The 2026 EX90 fixes most of the early-MY software pain via the 800V architecture upgrade and an OTA software baseline reset; reviewers welcome the new Single Motor RWD trim (significantly cheaper than the old Twin-only Twin Motor lineup) and the 670-hp Performance variant. KBB and Edmunds praise the cavernous interior, Bowers & Wilkins audio, and IIHS TSP+ rating, while still calling out infotainment quirks.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews on the 2026 EX90 are limited; 2024-2025 owners praised the cabin but cited charging, software, and 12V battery failures; OTA updates have addressed many.

notable_other: New for 2026: 800V electrical architecture (adds 155 mi in 10 min DC fast charge)
Pilot Assist, Park Pilot Assist standard

trims.notes: Base trim of EX90 lineup, new-for-2026 Single Motor RWD configuration (329 hp, 92 kWh battery, 291 mi range with 21" wheels). MSRP $76,695 (Volvo press release / KBB starting incl. destination) - $1,295 destination = $75,400 base. EPA fueleconomy.gov for Single Motor RWD with 21" wheels = id 50255 (291 mi, 92 MPGe combined). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ awarded (Large SUVs category). Standard 14.5-inch portrait tablet with Google built-in. Wireless Apple CarPlay supported.

changes: Adds second front motor: AWD-electric, 402 hp (vs. 329 hp RWD)
Upgrades battery from 92 kWh to 111 kWh (305 mi vs. 291 mi range)
Towing capacity rises to 4,850 lb (vs. 3,307 lb)

trims.notes: MSRP $79,995 (Volvo press release / KBB starting 7-pass incl. destination) - $1,295 destination = $78,700 base. 6-passenger configuration optional within trim. Images shared with ex90-standard family. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 50257 (Twin Motor with 21" wheels).

notable_other: Bowers & Wilkins 25-speaker premium audio with Dolby Atmos
Ventilated nappa leather seats with massage
Air suspension with active electronic shock absorbers

Upgrades battery to 111 kWh (305 mi range, 4,850 lb tow)
Adds Bowers & Wilkins 25-speaker Dolby Atmos audio
Adds ventilated nappa leather seats with massage

trims.notes: MSRP $84,345 (Volvo press release starting 7-pass incl. destination) - $1,295 destination = $83,050 base. Images shared with ex90-standard family.

notable_other: Twin Motor Performance: 670 hp, 642 lb-ft, 0-60 mph in 4.0 sec

trims.notes: Base trim of Twin Motor Performance line — 670 hp, the most powerful Volvo ever. Power upgrade from prior-year 510 hp Performance is significant for 2026. Separate powertrain line within EX90 model per spec §6.4 (different motor output spec than the 402 hp Twin Motor). EPA range 298 mi (slightly less than standard Twin Motor's 305 mi due to wheel/tire setup). MSRP $84,995 (Volvo press release starting 7-pass) - $1,295 destination = $83,700 base. EPA fueleconomy.gov id 50259 (Twin Motor Performance with 22" wheels).

notable_other: Ventilated nappa leather seats with massage
Soft-close doors and electrochromic glass roof

changes: Adds Bowers & Wilkins 25-speaker Dolby Atmos audio
Adds ventilated nappa leather seats with massage
Adds soft-close doors and electrochromic glass roof

trims.notes: MSRP $89,345 (Volvo press release / KBB starting 7-pass) - $1,295 destination = $88,050 base. Images shared with ex90-performance family. Twin Motor Performance Ultra is the lineup-topping trim at $88,050 base; higher figures cited in secondary coverage include optional packages and are not separate trims.

models.notes: EX90 has two powertrain lines per spec §6.4 distinction: (1) Twin Motor (with the new Single Motor RWD as its base, 402 hp Twin Motor as step-up) — these share the standard 510-hp-class drivetrain, and (2) Twin Motor Performance (670 hp, its own powertrain line). Single Motor Plus is the base of the standard line; Twin Motor Performance Plus is the base of the Performance line. 6-passenger captain's-chair configuration is optional within Plus/Ultra trims (treated as option per spec §6.5). For 2026 the EX90 moves to 800V electrical architecture (cuts DC charge time, adds 155 mi in 10 min). Won 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award (Large SUVs category). EPA fueleconomy.gov verified IDs: 50254/50255 for Single Motor RWD (20"/21" wheel variants), 50256/50257 for Twin Motor (20"/21"), 50258/50259 for Twin Motor Performance (21"/22"). Volvo's consumer site (volvocars.com/us) returned 403 to WebFetch; specs reconciled from EV Database, Volvo press release, KBB, Edmunds, and EPA.

models.generation_context: 1st-generation ES90 (SPA2 platform), unveiled March 2025 as Volvo's new all-electric flagship sedan and the brand's first 800-volt vehicle. Built in Daqing, China alongside the EX90. US deliveries began late 2025 / early 2026. The ES90 has a hatchback liftgate but Volvo markets it as a sedan; the body style is officially classified as a four-door sedan with liftback (similar to Audi A6 Sportback e-tron).
models.model_summary: All-electric flagship sedan (with hatchback liftgate) replacing the discontinued S90 in the US lineup. First Volvo with 800V architecture (350 kW peak DC fast charge). Offered in Single Motor RWD (329 hp), Twin Motor AWD (442 hp), and Twin Motor Performance (670 hp). WLTP range up to 435 miles; EPA range pending publication.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS for the Volvo brand: 296 PP100 (industry avg 204). Ranked 2nd to last in 2026 VDS. Driven heavily by infotainment/electronics problems in Recharge/EV lineup. Ranked 15th of 25 in CR 2026 brand rankings - middle of pack. XC60 PHEV reliability is below average. No 2026-MY model-level data is published for this specific model; assessment is based on brand-level signals.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Volvo ES90 or for the Volvo brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviews (international press, US first-look) praise the ES90's quiet, well-judged ride, premium cabin with Bowers & Wilkins audio, and 800V fast-charge speed. Reviewers consistently note the body straddles sedan/liftback styling and that interior space is among the best in the segment thanks to the EV-only packaging. Edmunds and KBB await US-spec test cars.

owner_reviews.summary: No US owner reviews yet — model is too new on the US market.

features.sound_system: Bowers & Wilkins 25-speaker premium audio (standard on Ultra; lower trims vary)
notable_other: Up to 350 kW DC fast charging (10-80% in ~20 min)
Google built-in with Snapdragon Cockpit Platform
Native NACS adapter included; CCS1 port standard

trims.notes: Base trim of ES90 lineup. ES90 first US deliveries late-2025/early-2026; Volvo has not officially announced final US MSRP for any trim — US pricing not published by manufacturer as of 2026-05-12 (MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources). Per spec §9 honesty rules, msrp_base and destination_fee are null pending confirmation — KBB, Edmunds, and CarsDirect have all noted pricing is unconfirmed (estimated $70-80K base, ~$120K top). EPA fueleconomy.gov has not yet published 2026 ES90 entries (not on the 2026 Volvo brand-make page as of research date); fuel_economy.city/highway/combined and EPA range left null per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback rule, with sources.fuel_economy pointed at the Volvo brand model-browse page. Single Motor RWD = 329 hp / 92 kWh battery / 88 kWh usable / 800V architecture / 350 kW peak DC charging. The ES90 specifically debuts as a liftback-styled sedan in the US after the conventional S90 was discontinued for 2026. NHTSA and IIHS have not rated the 2026 ES90 yet. Dimensions cited to volvocars.com/us/cars/es90-electric/ as the manufacturer product page (consumer site 403 to WebFetch but URL is canonical).

changes: Adds second motor: AWD-electric, 442 hp (vs. 329 hp RWD)
Upgrades battery from 92 kWh to 106 kWh (102 kWh usable)

trims.notes: US pricing not published by Volvo for any ES90 trim as of 2026-05-12; MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources. Per honesty rule, msrp_delta_usd null. Images shared with es90-standard family.

features.sound_system: Bowers & Wilkins 25-speaker premium audio with Dolby Atmos
notable_other: Twin Motor Performance: 670 hp, 671 lb-ft, 0-60 mph in 3.9 sec
Ventilated nappa leather seats with massage

trims.notes: Performance powertrain line base trim (separate from Single/Twin Motor standard line per spec §6.4). Singleton trim in its own family. 670 hp / 671 lb-ft / 3.9 sec 0-60 with 800V architecture. US pricing not published by Volvo as of 2026-05-12; MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources. EPA range pending publication.

models.notes: All-new ES90 enters US market for 2026 as Volvo's electric flagship sedan, replacing the discontinued S90. Two powertrain lines per spec §6.4: standard (Single Motor RWD base + Twin Motor AWD step-up) and Performance (Twin Motor Performance, sole-trim in singleton family). Body style classified as 'sedan' though it has a liftback hatch (Volvo's marketing language). 800V electrical architecture is Volvo's first in production. ES90 is built in Daqing, China alongside the EX90 (which is built in Charleston, SC). Volvo Car USA published a 2026 ES90 technical-data page on its media site (volvocars.com/us/media/models/es90/2026/specifications/) which returned 403 to WebFetch; specifications reconciled from Wikipedia (citing Volvo press), InsideEVs official-debut coverage, Edmunds, KBB. EPA has not yet published 2026 ES90 fueleconomy.gov entries — fuel_economy.city/highway/combined and ev_specifics.electric_range_mi/total_range_mi/mpge_combined values are null pending EPA test publication, with sources.fuel_economy pointed at the Volvo brand model-browse page per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. US MSRP not yet officially announced by Volvo as of 2026-05-12; msrp_base / destination_fee / msrp_range null per honesty rules — KBB and Edmunds carry preview pages with unspecified pricing. NHTSA and IIHS have not rated 2026 ES90.
